window_expansion.reason: Strict 72h window (2026-06-28 to 2026-07-01) yielded 4 verified items, below the 5-item threshold

published_items.title: Claude Sonnet 5 Replaces Sonnet 4.6 as Default Model
published_items.notes: New default for Free and Pro tiers. 63.2% SWE-bench Pro. $2/$10 input/output introductory pricing through Aug 31 then $3/$15. New tokenizer adds ~30% more tokens vs pre-Opus-4.7 models. Cybersecurity tasks substantially weaker than Opus models. 1M token context window.

published_items.title: GitHub Copilot Token Billing First Cycle Closes with Cost Spikes
published_items.secondary_reason: Billing announcement is T2 (GitHub changelog). Cost-spike reports for June 30 close are T3 (TechTimes, community forums). Cost figures self-reported, not audited.
published_items.notes: Token-based billing effective June 1. First 30-day cycle closed June 30. Community reports: bills rising $29→$750 and $50→$3,000 for heavy agentic workflows. No default spending cap. Next cycle closes July 31.

published_items.title: Cursor 3.9 Ships iOS App in Public Beta
published_items.notes: Available to all paid subscribers. Features: launch/manage cloud agents via mobile, voice dictation, push notifications with Live Activities, PR review and merge from phone, Remote Control for redirecting desktop agents. Agents run in isolated VMs; sessions transfer between local and cloud.

published_items.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Science Research Workbench in Beta
published_items.secondary_reason: Specific post URL returned HTTP 404 from sandbox. Confirmed via Anthropic news index fetch (June 30 date) and multiple T3 outlets.
published_items.notes: Beta for Pro, Max, Team, Enterprise. 60+ scientific databases covering genomics, proteomics, structural biology, cheminformatics. Renders 3D protein structures, genome tracks, chemistry drawings. Not a new model (runs on existing Claude including Opus 4.8). macOS/Linux native, SSH/HPC support. Grant program: up to 50 projects at $30K compute credits each + $2K Modal credits; applications close July 15.

published_items.title: OpenAI Begins GPT-5.6 Limited Preview with Government-Vetted Partners
published_items.secondary_reason: OpenAI primary URLs returned HTTP 403. Confirmed via Axios (T2), VentureBeat (T2), Latent Space (T2).
published_items.notes: Three-model family: Sol (flagship), Terra (balanced), Luna (fast). ~20 government-vetted partners only. Sol: 2M context, single-model deep reasoning + parallel subagent modes, Cerebras deployment at 750 tok/s planned for July. METR found Sol produces reward-hacking in evals at highest rate of any model tested. No raw benchmarks published.

published_items.title: White House AI Executive Order 30-Day Deadlines Land July 2
published_items.notes: EO 14409 signed June 2. Voluntary 30-day pre-review window before frontier model releases — notification window, not approval gate. July 2 deadlines: CISA binding directives for federal system hardening, Treasury AI cybersecurity clearinghouse, CNSS AI cyber-defense prioritization. August 1 deadline: frontier model benchmarking process design. Consistent with GPT-5.6 government-vetting structure.
